basic_details.job_title: GSSSB Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 08 Laboratory Assistant and Senior Laboratory Assistant Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB), Gandhinagar, has invited online applications for 8 Class-3 vacancies across five posts - Laboratory Assistant (Electrical), Laboratory Assistant (Electronics & Communication), Laboratory Assistant (Computer), Laboratory Assistant (Civil), and Senior Laboratory Assistant - under a Special Recruitment Drive exclusively for differently-abled (Divyang) candidates. Online applications are open from 22 June 2026 (2:00 PM) to 6 July 2026 (11:59 PM) at ojas.gujarat.gov.in, with the last date to apply being 6 July 2026.

application_fee.details: The fee must be paid only online - by Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallet - after the application is confirmed, and must be completed by 8 July 2026 (11:59 PM), after which the payment link closes automatically and no late fee will be accepted. A transaction fee applies in addition to the exam fee. Fees are refunded only to candidates who actually appear for the examination; candidates who do not pay the fee will have their application rejected without notice, and a candidate who skips the exam after paying will not get a refund.

qualification.details: Eligibility differs slightly by post in terms of educational qualification, but age limit, citizenship, and category rules are common across the four Laboratory Assistant posts (Senior Laboratory Assistant has a different age limit). | Citizenship: Candidates must be Indian citizens or possess the nationality prescribed under Rule-7 of the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. | Educational Qualification (as on 6 July 2026): | Post Required Qualification Laboratory Assistant (Electrical) Diploma in Electrical Engineering or Power Electronics Laboratory Assistant (Electronics & Communication) Diploma in Electronics & Communication Engineering Laboratory Assistant (Computer) Diploma in Computer Engineering or Information Technology Laboratory Assistant (Civil) Diploma in Civil Engineering Senior Laboratory Assistant Second-class Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Geology, or Industrial Chemistry | For all five posts , candidates additionally need basic knowledge of computer applications (as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment Rules, 1967) and adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi, or both. Candidates who do not currently hold a computer-knowledge certificate may still apply, but must produce it before appointment , failing which the appointing authority will cancel their selection. | Age Limit: For the four Laboratory Assistant posts, candidates must be not less than 18 years and not more than 33 years as on 6 July 2026. For Senior Laboratory Assistant, the upper limit is 35 years (also not less than 18 years). | Age Relaxation for Divyang Candidates: | Category Relaxation Maximum Age Limit General category Divyang - Male 10 years 43 years (Lab Asst posts) / 45 years (Sr. Lab Asst) General category Divyang - Female 15 years 45 years Reserved category Divyang - Male 15 years 45 years Reserved category Divyang - Female 20 years 45 years | Candidates already in Gujarat Government service may also get upper age relaxation as per the 1967 Recruitment Rules. Reserved-category candidates applying against an unreserved post will not get any age or other relaxation. SC/ST/SEBC/EWS Divyang candidates must submit category, non-creamy-layer, or income/asset certificates (as applicable) obtained within the window specified in the notification at the time of certificate verification; without these, they will be treated as general-category Divyang candidates.

salary_details.details: All four Laboratory Assistant posts carry a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000 for the first five years, after which satisfactory service entitles the candidate to regular appointment at 7th Pay Commission Level-2 (₹19,900–₹63,200). The Senior Laboratory Assistant post follows the same fixed-pay structure initially, moving to Level-4 (₹25,500–₹81,100) after five years. Both pay structures remain subject to the outcome of Supreme Court SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012. | Candidates with Benchmark Disability or a temporary disability affecting their ability to write may request a scribe and/or compensatory time (20 minutes extra per hour) for the exam. Candidates with Blindness or Locomotor disability (both arms affected) of 40% or more need only submit their disability certificate. Other eligible candidates must also submit a medical certificate (Appendix-I format) confirming their writing limitation, issued by the Chief Medical Officer/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent. Requests must be emailed to [email protected] at least 2 days before the exam, after the admit card download notice is published; instructions and forms will be available at https://gsssb.gujarat.gov.in/Home/ph. Requests received after this window will not be considered. | Photo/signature errors: Scan your photo and signature separately, save each as a JPG file within the prescribed size limit, and double-check dimensions before uploading - mismatched sizes are a common reason uploads fail. Payment gateway failures: If a transaction fails or hangs after the amount is deducted, wait and check your Payment Receipt status from the portal before retrying, rather than attempting a second payment immediately. Browser/device issues: Use an updated browser and a stable internet connection; clear cache if pages don't load correctly, and avoid submitting the form via mobile browsers if you face layout issues. Save your credentials: Note down your Application Number and Confirmation Number immediately after they are generated, and keep printed/saved copies of your application and fee receipt - these cannot be recovered easily later. Avoid last-day rush: Application portals see heavy traffic close to the deadline, which can cause slow loading or failed submissions - try to complete your application and fee payment well before the last date.

selection_process: Selection is conducted through a single-stage objective competitive examination (CBRT/OMR mode) consisting of two parts:
Part-A (90 marks): Logical Reasoning & Data Interpretation (30)
Quantitative Aptitude (30)
and Indian Constitution
Current Affairs
and Gujarati & English Comprehension (30) Part-B (120 marks): Questions related to the candidate's subject/post
A combined total of 210 questions for 210 marks are asked

with a combined duration of 3 hours (180 minutes). Negative marking applies - ¼ mark is deducted for every wrong answer. A minimum qualifying standard of 40% applies independently to both Part-A and Part-B for all candidates.
After the exam
GSSSB will release a provisional answer key
invite objections
and then publish the final answer key
followed by the provisional result. Candidates within roughly twice the number of category-wise vacancies (based on merit

subject to qualifying marks) will be called for document verification. The final selection list and waiting list are then prepared category- and disability-group-wise based on merit. The notification does not state exam dates
result timelines
or interview details beyond these stages.
Widowed women candidates and candidates who have represented in recognised national/international
inter-university
or All India School-level sports get a 5% bonus on their marks for merit purposes
subject to producing the required certificates at verification.
how_to_apply: Under "Online Application," click Apply and select GSSSB.
Click on the relevant advertisement number/post name, then tap Apply; review the More Details option before proceeding, then click Apply now.
In the new window, click Skip to open the Application Format and fill in Personal Details (fields marked with a red asterisk * are mandatory).
Accept the conditions under Assurance by selecting "Yes," then click Save to complete the form and generate your Application Number - save this number.

Click Upload Photograph, enter your application number and birth date, then upload your photo and signature. Photo: 5 cm height × 3.6 cm width; Signature: 2.5 cm height × 7.5 cm width - both in JPG format, not exceeding 15 KB. The photo must be a recent, passport-size, white-background photo taken within 1 year of the last date to apply.

Click Confirm Application, enter your application number and birth date, review all details carefully, and edit if needed - no changes are possible after confirmation. Confirming generates a Confirmation Number, which you must save.

Click Print Application, select the job, enter your confirmation number and birth date, and print a copy of your application for your records (this can only be downloaded until the last date to apply).

Submit only one application. If multiple applications are submitted, the last confirmed application with the fee paid will be treated as valid, and others will be rejected; fees paid against rejected applications are not refunded.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for GSSSB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 6 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. The application fee can be paid until 8 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to apply for these posts?
faq.answer: Only differently-abled (Divyang) candidates holding a valid disability certificate matching the specific disability category notified for each post are eligible; this is a Special Recruitment Drive exclusively for such candidates.

faq.question: Q3. How many total vacancies are there?
faq.answer: There are 8 vacancies in total: 2 each for Laboratory Assistant (Electrical, Computer, and Civil), and 1 each for Laboratory Assistant (Electronics & Communication) and Senior Laboratory Assistant.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee?
faq.answer: Unreserved candidates (Laboratory Assistant posts) pay ₹500, while reserved-category candidates, including all Divyang candidates, pay ₹400. The fee is refunded only to candidates who appear for the exam.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit?
faq.answer: For the four Laboratory Assistant posts, candidates must be 18–33 years as on 6 July 2026; for Senior Laboratory Assistant, the upper limit is 35 years. Age relaxation of 10–20 years applies to Divyang candidates depending on category and gender.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process?
faq.answer: Selection is through a single-stage MCQ-based written exam (Part-A: 90 marks, Part-B: 120 marks) with negative marking, followed by document verification and a category-wise merit/selection list.

faq.question: Q7. What is the official website to apply?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online at https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in; recruitment-related notices are published at https://gsssb.gujarat.gov.in.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for these posts?
faq.answer: All posts carry a fixed pay of ₹26,000/month for the first 5 years. Thereafter, Laboratory Assistant posts move to Level-2 (₹19,900–₹63,200) and Senior Laboratory Assistant moves to Level-4 (₹25,500–₹81,100).

faq.question: Q9. What educational qualification is required?
faq.answer: A relevant diploma in engineering (Electrical, Electronics & Communication, Computer, or Civil) is required for the Laboratory Assistant posts, while Senior Laboratory Assistant requires a second-class Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Geology, or Industrial Chemistry.

faq.question: Q10. Is a scribe facility available for Divyang candidates?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates with Benchmark Disability affecting their writing ability can request a scribe and/or 20 minutes of compensatory time per hour by emailing GSSSB at least 2 days before the exam.
